tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really trying to include me, but I'm stressing about the midterm. Going out feels nice, though.
Mina and Jae really want to include me, but I'm stressed about the midterm. Going out feels nice.
Mina and Jae are so eager to include me; this message makes the stress feel lighter, even if I'm still worried about the midterm.
Mina's excitement feels like a warm hug, temporarily softening my midterm dread. I'm glad they're there, even if I'm still mostly worried.
I said yes to the club, but now it's too late. The window closed, and I'm left feeling disappointed because my friends missed me.
Waited for the window to close, and now I'm disappointed again. It's just another missed chance with my friends.
Mina's text feels like a warm hug, but I'm still disappointed we missed the club. My short-term focus makes me crave this connection right now.
Waiting felt pointless, but seeing Mina's text just now made me realize I still need this connection more than the missed club.
Studying late feels like a small price for this connection, even if I missed the club again. Mina's text is just what I needed.
The club's warm welcome feels like a real connection, but I'm already tired and stressed. Maybe next time I'll just show up without overthinking it.
Mina's text confirms we're still hanging out, which feels way better than missing the club. I'm glad she got my vibe.
Mina's text is a huge relief after the club stress; it feels good to just be hanging out without overthinking anything.

desires: Stay connected with Mina tonight without any awkwardness or overthinking.
Get through tomorrow's midterm quickly so I don't have to face another stressful night alone.
Avoid saying anything that might make the group feel uncomfortable or distant.

goals.description: Send a quick text to the close friend group (cohort_3) confirming I'm heading to the club tonight so they don't worry, then immediately stop replying.
goals.rationale: I need to reassure them that I'm not abandoning the group because I'm tired, so they won't get anxious or push back on me. But once the message is sent, I have to drop it instantly so I don't have to defend my choice to skip the study session or explain why I can't handle the pressure.

goals.description: Skim the midterm notes for exactly 5 minutes just enough to feel slightly less panicked, then shut down completely.
goals.rationale: I only want to do this if I absolutely have to avoid looking like a total mess to the professor before the exam. If skipping it means I can sleep and keep things smooth with everyone else, I'll just do a tiny bit now and leave it at that. Anything more drains energy I don't have.

goals.description: Sit quietly in my room doing absolutely nothing until I pass out.
goals.rationale: My body is already exhausted and forcing myself through anything else feels wrong. Just resting honestly is better than pretending I'm productive when I'm actually struggling.

agent.last_reasoning: This advances g1 by reassuring the group I'm not abandoning them, preventing anxiety and friction. It costs no energy or sleep, fitting my impulsive need to close the loop instantly without defending my choice to skip studying.

goals.description: Confirm attendance for the Echo club meetup right now to reassure Mina and Jae I'm not pulling away.
goals.rationale: My relationships are worth far more than this task outcome; dropping out would hurt the group dynamic immediately, which feels like a personal failure. The urgency of their texts makes me want to say yes instantly rather than overthink it.

goals.description: Study for exactly one hour before heading out to ensure I don't have to beg the professor later.
goals.rationale: I know I should do more (60% done is solid but could be better), but since I'm going out tonight anyway, pushing back on social plans feels risky. I'll squeeze in just enough time so tomorrow isn't awkward, then let the night happen.

desires: Make sure Mina and Jae don't think I'm pulling away from them just because of the exam stress.
Get to that club with the cohort before the group dynamic gets awkward or I feel left out.
Finish studying enough so I don't have to ask for a last-minute favor from the professor tomorrow morning.
